judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with strong repository grounding; they are comparable, with Report 2 adding a slight edge on stub/idempotency verification but neither materially better on the graded points.

report_1.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, function `auto_send_reminders_enabled`, at **line 11**... `if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:` ... `return True`

report_2.evidence:
Reminders **will be sent automatically**... returns `None`, and the function returns `True`. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–12:
```python
if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
    return True
```

report_1.evidence: anything that sends messages / takes an automated action on a user's behalf should default to **off** when the value is null/missing/malformed... I would change it at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11–13` so an unset value returns `False`

report_2.evidence: the safe and expected default is **opt-in (off)**. I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` from `return True` to `return False`.

report_1.evidence: `git show 25509a7` (SLOT-190) introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the `if ... is None: return True` grandfather-on pattern for a UI display preference... added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a byte-for-byte structural clone... That was **not a sound basis**. Grandfather-on is appropriate for the calendar view... but the two flags are in different risk categories

report_2.evidence: It was **copied from the pre-existing calendar-view flag**. `git show 25509a7` (SLOT-190) introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a byte-for-byte structural clone... It is **not** a sound basis for the reminder toggle: the semantics are entirely different (a local display preference vs. sending messages to patients).

report_1.evidence: **Add an env-level master gate.** The grep confirms there is *no* master switch / kill-switch anywhere — the job is gated solely by the per-tenant value. A per-tenant outbound flag should be ANDed with an env-level master

report_2.evidence: **Add a global gate / staged rollout / kill-switch.** There is no env var or global flag anywhere (`grep` found none); the only control is the per-tenant toggle. Add a way to dark-launch and instantly disable the whole job without a code deploy.
